creator: arthurbarlow@earthlink.net
text:
I'm using a seven year old Gateway PC that has all Intel drivers, (video, audio, agp, pci, isa).  When I run "X -configure" it picks the "intel" video driver.  The chip set is ntel 82810-DC100 GMCH.  When I run it I get the following error in Xorg.0.log:

Error in I810WaitLpRing(), now is 2371005, start is 2369004
pgetbl_ctl: 0x12a0001 pgetbl_err: 0x0
ipeir: 0 iphdr: 80f00a00
LP ring tail: 2688 head: 19c len: f001 start 370000
eir: 0 esr: 1 emr: 3d
instdone: ff7a instpm: 0
memmode: 4 instps: 810
hwstam: 9ac7 ier: 0 imr: 9ac7 iir: 0
space: 56076 wanted 65528

fatal server error:
lockup

How-To-Repeat: Happens anytime you run Xorg with "intel" video driver.
